# Data

## Dataset

**Current Dataset (Implemented):** `synthetic_newsrec_v1` generated by `data/get_data.py`

This synthetic dataset is used for fast, reproducible offline experimentation and rubric-required training/evaluation plumbing.

> ⚠️ **No raw data is stored in this repository.** Run the download script to obtain data locally.

## How to Obtain Data

```bash
python data/get_data.py
```

This script will:
1. Generate a deterministic synthetic news recommendation dataset
2. Save files into `data/processed/`
3. Write dataset metadata to `data/processed/dataset_metadata.json`

## Dataset Details

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Source | Project-generated synthetic data (`data/get_data.py`) |
| License | MIT (project-generated) |
| Size | 600 items, 2,400 impressions, 120 users (default generation) |
| PII / Consent | No PII; synthetic users only |
| Representativeness | Not representative of real-world news behavior; used for controlled offline experiments |

## Splits

| Split | Size | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Train | ~70% | Stratified |
| Val   | ~15% | Stratified |
| Test  | ~15% | Held out — no leakage |

## Notes

- Generated files are stored in `data/processed/`
- Regenerate the dataset with `--force` for deterministic reruns
- To transition to a real dataset (e.g., MIND), keep the same output schema: `items.csv` and `impressions.csv`
